# About us

Welcome to Eastern Tea Traders, your trusted destination for authentic and premium Assam teas. Rooted in the lush tea gardens of Assam, we bring you the finest handpicked leaves, carefully sourced to ensure unmatched freshness, flavor, and aroma.

At Eastern Tea Traders, tea is not just a beverage – it’s a tradition, a culture, and a way of life. Our mission is to share the richness of Assam’s tea heritage with tea lovers across India and beyond. From strong and malty Assam black tea to soothing green and herbal blends, every cup we offer tells a story of purity and craftsmanship.

title: Wholesale Organic Assam & Green Tea from Small Farmers
content:
With providing additional information we want to make sure you will be able to fulfill the needs of your customers and give them the opportunity to learn about the authentic world of Assam tea.

### PAYMENT

Payment is possible via Bank Transfer and PayPal. 

### INFORMATION NEEDED FOR WHOLESALE OPTION

To determine the eligibility of our wholesale customers we will need to ask you for your information.

 First and Last name
 Company name
 Email address
 Address
 Postal code
 Phone number
 Business Registration Number
 Short Description of your business
 Web site URL
 Which import documentations do you need

Feel free to share with us your specific requirements and contact us for any additional questions.

## Subscribe to our emails [...] Wholesale offer is not open for individuals that wish to buy small quantities of less than minimum order quantity.

### MINIMUM ORDER QUANTITY

Our minimum order quantity for wholesale is 5 kg. Larger quantities are available upon request. Please contact us to determine your specific needs.

### PRICING

Once you send us an inquiry about wholesale orders and we determine your eligibility, you will receive a price list of our organic artisan teas. Wholesale prices will include taxes.  

### SAMPLES

15 gram samples of our teas are available for wholesale clients. Please keep in consideration that we need your full details to determine purpose of your inquiry. Samples might be subject to further shipping charges.

### SHIPPING AND DELIVERY TIME [...] Learn more about the importance of organic farming.

## WHOLESALE GUIDE

### CHOOSE YOUR TEA GRADE

Sometimes it may seem difficult to choose from so many tea varieties and tea leaves. We suggest that you determine the needs of your customers before making a purchase of larger quantities of tea. If you are not sure which leaf to choose, we will be happy to assist you in the evaluation process. We are offering black tea leaves of different classification, including loose leaf, TGFOP and FTGFOP.

### ELIGIBILITY

Wholesale pricing and orders are available for tea shops, businesses and individuals who agree on buying the minimum wholesale quantity of tea. If you are considering opening a tea business, please feel free to contact us to get more information about our wholesale offer.

title: Top 10 Most Famous Tea Estates in Assam | History of Ceylon Tea
content: Nestled in the serene Baksa district of Assam, Nagrijuli Tea Estate offers a unique blend of natural beauty and quality tea. The estate's name is derived from the local term "Narchi-Juri," which translates to "eternal spring." This name perfectly reflects the estate's tea, which is refreshing and stimulating like the perpetual spring. Nagrijuli Tea Estate is renowned for its production of both Orthodox and CTC teas, both of which are of exceptional quality. The estate's favorable climatic conditions and diverse flora and fauna make it an ideal location for tea cultivation. The tea manufacturing process has evolved with time, with selected plants' cuttings now being used for planting. In addition to the tea estate's picturesque charm, Baksa district's other popular tourist attractions, [...] charm, Baksa district's other popular tourist attractions, including the Manas National Park and Tiger Project, Moina Pukhuri, and Bogamati, add to its allure. Nagrijuli Tea Estate's tranquil surroundings and premium tea make it a must-visit destination for nature and tea enthusiasts alike. [...] Located in the picturesque district of Moran in Assam, the Halmari Tea Estate is a renowned tea producer owned by the Daga family. With a total area of 374 hectares, the estate has been in operation for over a century, perfecting the art of tea production. It is popular not only in Assam but across the country for its remarkable quantity of tea variants, which are available for purchase throughout the year via online shopping, ensuring a vast clientele base. The estate caters to the domestic as well as the international markets with the highest quality tea, making it one of the top tea producers and traders globally. To maintain this status, Halmari employs highly skilled and experienced specialists for the proper processing of tea, starting from the plucking phase to the packaging phase.

Neither our business objectives nor our innovation goals stop at increasing yield and improving quality. As significantly, we strive to ensure the wellbeing of our farmers and their families.

Working towards that, one of our most recent endeavours was introducing Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Smart Drones to our plantation process.

title: Popular Tea Gardens to Visit in Assam, North East India
content:
The Nagrijuli Tea Estate is widely renowned for its two famous products which are CTC (crush, tear, curl) tea and the unique Orthodox tea formula. The climate and ambiance of the area are perfect for the premium quality tea leaves. Tourists visiting Assam for tea garden tours can also enjoy other attractions apart from the tea estates such as national parks and mountain ranges.

The Manas National Park is situated close to the Nagrijuli National Park and can be visited easily by the well-constructed roadways. For those who love natural surroundings and beverages, Nagrijuli Tea Estate is an absolute must-see for them because of its peaceful surroundings and exquisite tea.

Location: No.2.Piplani, Baksa district, Assam 781368

### Harmutty Tea Estate [...] Location: Manohari Tea Estate, Nogaghuli 1/177 Orr, Assam 786012

### Nagrijuli Tea Estate

Nagrijuli Tea Estate Assam

Nagrijuli Tea Estate, located in the Baksa district of Assam covers an area of over 604 acres and tends to produce unique and quality tea to its customers. The area is home to the local Bodo community and in the local language, Nagrijuli is derived from the word ‘Narchi-Juri’ which means eternal spring. The spring that surrounds the estate offers clear and refreshing water to the tea plants. [...] Popular Tea Gardens to Visit in Assam | Tea Tourism in Assam

 Call +91 9212553107

Must Visit Popular Tea Gardens in Assam

#### 28 Feb, 2024

# Popular Tea Gardens to Visit in Assam, North East India

India boasts the title of producing tea of the highest quality and distinct varieties. All types of tea leaves are grown in India yet the Black Tea takes the crown, particularly in the northeast part of India. Well, thanks to India’s unique topography, varied climatic conditions, and ample strength to produce tea leaves every year in huge amounts. Assam, the second largest state of Northeast India is blessed with natural beauty, lush tea gardens, overflowing wildlife, serene Brahmaputra River and the warmness of indigenous people there.

## About Baksa, Assam, India [...] ## About Baksa, Assam, India

Baksa is a district in Assam, India, and is considered part of Bodoland, a region in Northwest Assam inhabited by the Bodo people. It was created in 2003 as part of the Barpeta, Nalbari, and Kamrup districts, in the end of an armed conflict that created the Bodoland Territorial Council, a semi-autonomous region in Assam. It is bordered to the east by Udalguri.  
   
 Baksa grows some tea; Doomni estate is located here. Some older sources mention the older district names when describing the location of tea gardens in this region.

# Tea Estates

DPG group has grown consistently through its decisive and visionary response to changing times. It has established lush Longtong Tea Estate, Changlang Tea Estate and has set up verdant and green smaller tea gardens in the rich, fertile regions of Upper Assam and Arunachal Pradesh.

The group has developed unmatched expertise in Tea manufacturing by setting up six tea processing units, within the green lush tea growing estates of upper Assam. These state-of-the-art manufacturing units have the capacity to produce close to almost 10 kgs of delicious tea per annum.

title: Indian Trade Portal
content:
aboutUsIndian Trade Portal

# Tea Industry

Tea Industry and Exports in India

INTRODUCTION [...] EXPORT TREND - TOTAL PRODUCT

India is among the top 5 tea exporters in the world making about 10% of the total exports. In the year 2021, the total value of tea exports from India was around US$ 687.9 million. Indian Assam, Darjeeling, and Nilgiri tea are considered one of the finest in the world. The majority of the tea exported out of India is black tea which makes up about 96% of the total exports. The types of tea exported through India are: Black tea, Regular tea, Green tea, Herbal tea, Masala tea and Lemon tea. Out of these, black tea, regular tea and green tea make up approximately 80%, 16% and 3.5% of the total tea exported from India. [...] The northern part of India is the biggest producer at about 83% of the country's annual tea production in 2021-22 with the majority of the production coming from Assam followed by West Bengal. The Assam valley and Cachar are the two tea producing regions in Assam. In West Bengal, Dooars, Terai and Darjeeling are the three major tea producer regions. The southern part of India produces about 17% of the country's total production with the major producing states being Tamil Nadu, Kerala, and Karnataka.

EXPORT TREND - TOTAL PRODUCT

# Assam Tea Export from India - A Simple Guide

• By Export Business Mart

Assam Tea Export from India - A Simple Guide

Assam Tea Export Guide

# Assam Tea Export from India is a Growing Opportunity in the Global Tea Market.

India has long been recognized for its great tea tradition, with Assam tea as an important part of the international marketplace. Assam tea is known for its bold flavor, deep color and rich aroma, it has become one of the most popular varieties of tea in many countries (USA, UAE, Russia, UK and Germany). Today, there is demand for a great quantity of high quality Indian teas, therefore creating a large and growing industry for Assam Tea Export from India. [...] | Tea Product | HSN Code |
 --- |
| Green Tea in Packings | 09021010 |
| Fermented Black Tea in Packings | 09023010 |
| Bulk Assam Black Teas in Bulk | 09024020 |

This HSN code is essential for the customs clearance process, completion of export documents, GST filers and for use in international trade when exporting tea from Assam to destinations outside of India.

## How to Start an Assam Tea Business as an Exporter from India

Assam tea may be a lucrative business opportunity if your plan is well thought out. Below are the steps to take when starting an Assam tea business. [...] 4. Does the Assam Tea export from India business make money?  
 Yes, the Assam Tea export business is very lucrative because of the ever-increasing demand for specialized teas and the high price paid for these teas by buyers.

5. What paperwork do you need to export tea from India?  
 To export tea from India you will need an IE Code (IEC), a GST Registration, a Tea Board Registration and an Export Certificate (depending on the importing country).

6. Who is the best way for international buyers to find Indian exporters of Assam Tea?  
 International buyers can find export grade Assam Tea suppliers from India via B2B marketplaces such as Export Business Mart. Other ways include using trade directories or tea industry exhibitions.

## Product Overview

### COMPREHENSIVE PRODUCT OVERVIEW

### INDIAN TEA EXPORTS — A GLOBAL HERITAGE

Indian tea is among the best known in the world. India is the fourth largest tea exporter globally, with exports reaching over 535 million kg annually. Tea is grown in several renowned states across India, including the Nilgiris, Assam, and Darjeeling. Not only is India one of the largest tea exporters, but it is also one of the largest consumers worldwide. At IndianXporter, we specialize in exporting a wide range of premium Indian teas directly from these legendary estates. [...] ### TEA VARIETIES WE EXPORT

We export an extensive variety of teas including Black Tea, Green Tea, Ginger Tea, Cardamom Tea, Kashmiri Tea, Butter Tea, and Masala Chai. We export to various countries across the European Union, the United States, and the Middle East. Beyond their beautiful flavors, aromas, and textures, our teas offer numerous health benefits. Rich in antioxidants, tea supports heart health, boosts the immune system and metabolism, and aids in weight management and bone strength.

### DARJEELING TEA

Darjeeling tea is famous across the globe and is known for its powerful antioxidant qualities. Grown exclusively in the Darjeeling district of West Bengal, it possesses a unique flavor and quality that cannot be replicated anywhere else in the world.

### ASSAM TEA [...] ### QUALITY ASSURANCE & EXPORT PROCESSING

At IndianXporter, quality is our foremost priority. From the point of origin to the final dispatch port, Premium Indian Tea undergoes rigorous multi-stage quality control. Our processing facilities utilize advanced sorting, grading, and packaging machinery. Every batch is certified for export, guaranteeing compliance with ISO, FDA, and destination-specific safety regulations.

As a leading manufacturer and exporter in India, IndianXporter ensures that our Premium Indian Tea meets the stringent quality requirements of international markets. Our robust supply chain allows us to fulfill bulk orders consistently, offering highly competitive FOB and CIF pricing to our B2B partners worldwide.

### Coronavirus

Coronavirus Image

View More [...] Assam is not just the largest contiguous tea-growing area in the world. The Tocklai Experimental Station in Jorhat district of Assam is the world’s oldest and largest research station of its kind, carries out clonal propagation and constant research so that the strength of the full-bodied liquor is retained and the tea bushes yield high quality tea.

Both Orthodox and CTC (Crush/Tear/Curl) varieties of tea are manufactured here. Assam Orthodox Tea is a registered Geographical Indication (GI).

The Guwahati Tea Auction Centre (GTAC) is one of the busiest tea trading facilities in the world. It is located in Guwahati and its primary commodity under the hammer is Assam tea.It was established in 1970. It has seen the largest volume of CTC tea auction in the world. [...] Where to buy Assam Tea from?

Assam Tea is available across the state and in the shelves of most of the supermarkets across the world. You can purchase Assam Tea online from a variety of grocery shopping websites. Still if you happen to be in Guwahati, find below the list of places to buy Assam Tea from:

title: About Us – Assam Tea Estate
content:
Our goal is to support businesses with high-quality Assam tea at competitive wholesale prices.

## Why Choose Assam Tea Estates?

We believe that every cup of tea should deliver happiness, freshness, and authentic Assam flavor.

### Direct sourcing from Assam tea gardens

### ISO certified quality standards

### Lab tested tea quality

### Organic and safe tea products

### Reliable bulk and wholesale supply

### Premium taste and strong aroma

### Our Mission

Our mission is to promote the authentic heritage of Assam tea and deliver premium tea products that combine quality, tradition, and purity.

## Contact Us

 129 Sachal Path, VIP Road, Guwahati, Assam – 781022 [...] We offer a wide range of premium tea varieties:

 Kashmiri Tea – A unique and royal tea blend
 Premium Loose Tea - Finest quality loose leaf Assam tea

Each tea blend is carefully selected to maintain consistent taste, freshness, and aroma.

## Bulk & Wholesale Tea Supply

Assam Tea Estate is also a reliable bulk tea supplier and wholesale tea distributor.

We provide:

 Bulk loose tea supply
 Wholesale tea orders
 Custom tea blending
 Private label tea packaging
 Tea supply for shops, cafes, and distributors

title: Tea Brokers and Tea Traders - UK Tea & Infusions Association
content:
Founded by a descendant of Earl Grey, we offer only the most distinctive teas from areas of the world renown for their tea: wonderful orchid-like black teas from the distant provinces of China; beautiful Oolongs from Formosa; malty Assams, muscatel Darjeelings or fragrant Nilgiris from the southern Blue mountains of India.  We have healthy Green or exotic White teas from China, India, Kenya and Japan.  We also offer classic blends.

Retail orders over £30 are delivered free in the UK.

Jing Tea Limited

Jing Tea Limited  
 325 Canterbury Court  
1-3 Brixton Road  
 London  
 SW9 6DE

Jing Tea Limited logo

INTRODUCTION [...] We pride ourselves that quality, service and consistency are at the heart of what we do  and we are always happy to enthusiastically discuss tea with both our regular customers and potential customers.

Varieties of Tea Offered:

Orthodox loose leaf teas; Black; Green; White & Oolong. Orthodox produced flavoured Black; Green & Oolong teas.

Herbal and Fruit Infusions

Ayurveda Infusions.

Forthcoming Tea Events:

Twice a year we host tea tasting workshops at our head office in Somerset. These events are hugely popular with tea enthusiasts and, especially, for those who are planning to start up a tea related business. For more details and information please visit our website: www.cupoftea.co.uk or call our offices on 01761 239162. [...] Flowering Tea: When it comes to our flowering or blooming teas, they are not just pretty to look at, but are also a delight to drink.

Oolong Tea: From Tie Guan Yin, China?s most popular Oolong tea to DarjOolong, a rare Oolong from Darjeeling you can find them at Tea People.

Herbal & Fruit Infusions: We boast of a wide range of some unique herbal and fruit infusions such as  Liquorice Mint Toffee, Spicy Apple, Very Berry, etc.

Rooibos: We stock an exclusive range of Rooibos or Red Bush tea. Our Choco Mint Rooibos won 2 gold stars at the Great Taste Awards 2014.

## Assam CTC

### Description

Tea: Assam Pekoe

Grower: Heritage Tea Assam

Teamaker: Ishan Baruah of Heritage Tea Assam

Origin: Dibrugarh in Assam, India

Elevation: 100m (350ft)

Cultivation: Natural (Organic, but no certification)

Harvest Date: Autumn 2016

• • •

Classic Assam profile with a robust, malty flavor. Soft rose undertones.

• • •

The region of Assam is located at low elevation, with abundant rainfall and high humidity. This results in robust, malty teas. Traditionally, the area produces low-grade CTC and fannings for the commodity market, but Heritage Tea Assam focuses on quality teas. [...] Assam CTC is crafted from tea leaves harvested by hand from Camellia sinensis assamica (large-leaf) bushes. Unlike many other tea pickers in Assam, who focus on quantity rather than quality, the pluckers at Heritage Tea Assam are well-versed in good plucking standards.

Heritage Tea grows with locally-sourced vermicompost, and is completely chemical-free. The founder, Rajen, keeps detailed notes on each tea and educates local growers and pickers, allowing for continued improvement.

Learn about the people behind this tea: Heritage Tea Assam

• • •

title: Bongaigaon, Assam, India - Teas
content:
Bongaigaon(বঙাইগাঁও in Assamese) is a district in lower Assam, India. It stretches from Bongaigaon south to the north banks of the Brahmaputra river, and includes the Manas river where it flows into the Brahmaputra. Bongaigaon is b ordered to the west by Dhubri and to the south by Goalpara.  
   
 It was formed in 1989 out of parts of Goalpara and Kokrajhar districts, but in 2004 was reduced in size as part of it was taken to make the newer Chirang district. As such, maps of Assam don't always consistently reflect this district's current size and location and older maps don't display it at all. Sources writing about tea may handle tea grown in this district inconsistently because of the changing boundaries.  
   
 Bongaigaon grows some tea, which is occasionally available in the West. [...] Show Menu)

title: Tea Farming in India, Top Tea Manufacturers & Exporters in India - IBEF
content:
Besides the development and regulatory framework, the Tea Board of India also undertakes direct promotional activities, including organizing joint participation in international fairs and exhibitions, arranging buyer and seller meets, and sending & hosting trade delegations. It also conducts market surveys, analyses, identifies, tracks consumer behaviour, and provides relevant and accurate information to importers and exporters.

##### Partners

Swatch Bharat [...] Assam Valley and Cachar are the two tea-producing regions in Assam. In West Bengal, Dooars, Terai and Darjeeling are the three major tea producer regions. The southern part of India produces about 17% of the country's total production with the major producing states being Tamil Nadu, Kerala, and Karnataka.

## Export Trend

India is among the top 5 tea exporters in the world making about 10% of the total exports. In FY26 (April-July 2025), the total value of tea exports from India stood at US$ 314 million. Indian Assam, Darjeeling, and Nilgiri tea are considered one of the finest in the world. Majority of the tea exported out of India is black tea which makes up about 96% of the total exports. title: Wholesale Tea Supplier in India | Bulk CTC, Private Label & Café Supply — Palak Tea Co
content:
## Why Buy Wholesale Tea From Palak Tea Co?

Palak Tea Co is one of India's most experienced wholesale tea suppliers, operating from Siliguri, West Bengal — the heart of India's tea trade corridor. For over 35 years, we have been sourcing CTC, Orthodox, Green, White, and Herbal teas directly from over 400 tea gardens across Assam and the Dooars region. Our direct-from-garden model eliminates middlemen and auction markups, saving our wholesale buyers ₹15 to ₹20 per kilogram compared to standard market rates. [...] # Bulk CTC Tea for Wholesalers, Cafés & Private Labels — Save ₹15-20/kg

Direct from 400+ Assam & Dooars gardens. 1000+ cups tasted daily so every batch matches the last. 35 years serving wholesalers, café chains, private label brands and exporters across India and 5 continents.

## Request Wholesale Tea Pricing & Samples

Fill in the form below. Our sales team will contact you within 24 hours with pricing, samples, and all the details you need.

We respond to every enquiry within 24 hours — by call or WhatsApp

## Bulk Tea for Every Business

From single-grade CTC for wholesalers to private label manufacturing for new brands — we serve every segment of the tea trade. Every product backed by 35 years of garden relationships, a strict 3-step quality check, and dispatch within 24-48 hours. [...] Garden sample → purchase sample → physical bag sample. Every single batch. 1000+ cups tasted daily by our expert taster Rahul Jhawar. No shortcuts on quality.

We maintain huge stock volumes year-round. Orders dispatched within 24-48 hours. Pan-India delivery in 7-8 days via GATI, TCI, Bluedart, Delhivery, VRL.

Custom blends for your region. Private labelling for your brand. We make the tea your market wants — from family blends to premium hotel blends.

CTC (BOP, BOPSM, BP, PD, OF, Dust), leaf tea, green, herbal, flavoured, flower teas. Single grade or blended. Assam or Dooars. Every price range.

UPI, bank transfer, credit card — no hassles. Start with a sample order. Scale when you're ready. Bulk discounts on large orders.

title: Tea Plantation - FIBIwiki
content:
## Historical books

 The British Library has the following books in its catalogue:
  + Taylor’s Maps of the following Tea Districts, Darjeeling, Terai, Jalpaiguri and Dooars, Darrang, Golaghat, Jorhat, Nowgong, Sibsagar, Lakhimpur, Dibrugarh, Cachar, Sylhet, with complete Index to all Tea Gardens, published 1910. Consists of 11 Plates/Maps. UIN: BLL01004862801
  + Tales and Songs from an Assam Tea Garden by Maurice P. Hanley (Calcutta 1928) UIN: BLL0100158619
  + The Trials of a Planter by Oscar Lindgren (Kalimpong 1933) UIN: BLL01002174145
  + Assam Planter: Tea Planting and Hunting in the Assam Jungle by A. R. Ramsden. (London 1945) UIN: BLL01009605678

## Historical books online [...] Very interesting and detailed interviews of many aspects of the life and work of a tea planter. Travancore State, Calcutta, Darjeeling, N.W.F.P. Recorded by A.S. Robertson and his son, A.F. Robertson (1976 and 1979) from University of Cambridge - Centre of South Asian Studies. Listen to the interviews, or read the transcripts.
 Assam Where? Growing up in the tea growing district of Cachar during the late 1940s and the 1950s from Shangrilajournals.com. (There are links at the bottom of the page)
 [Halycon [sic] days: a memoir of tea estate life]( by Duncan Allan (archived))
 "It's time for tea" by Anurag & Priya Ganapathy. Supplement, Deccan Sunday Herald (possibly Sunday 22 June 2014), now an archived webpage. An overview. [...] ## Contents

## Fibis Resources

 Tea Planters Cachar 1865-1875 on the FIBIS database, over 200 names listed.
 FIBIS Journal Number 9, "Jokai Tea Estates" by Dick Barton. Includes a useful reading list.
 FIBIS Journal Number 24, "Life with Tea in India: The Diaries of Samuel Cleland Davidson" by Wendy Pratt and Peter Bleakley
 Tea Images Images relating to tea planters and tea production comprising some of the original material mentioned below - examples of which are on this page.

## Orthodox Black Tea from Assam, India

‹ ›

### Description

Assam Orthodox Teas are rolled with machinery in a manner that mimics hand-rolling. Most specialty tea is made with orthodox production methods. Orthodox tea is generally known as the champagne of teas as they are more nuanced and complex than other teas. All whole-leaf tea is made with orthodox production methods. Heritage Orthodox Teas are known for its distinctive flavor and taste.

### How To Brew [...] ### How To Brew

Use 2 grams (1 teaspoon dried tea leaves) for 8 fl. oz. water 205F/96C in your favorite teapot or infuser (use bottled drinking water for best flavor, let boil and steep at 205F). Steep for 1 minute. Leaves may be infused at least 3 times.

These are suggested brewing instructions. We encourage you to experiment and share your own brewing methods as you continue to grow in the tea culture. If you're new to tea this is a good way to start.

### Ghatia Tea Estate :

Ghatia Tea Estate is in North Bengal in the Dooars belt, just below the foothills of Bhutan. It has a mild undulating topography with a gentle slope from North to South. Being on the foothold of Bhutan it enjoys a cooler climate which imparts the extra flavour that is normally missing in the Dooars tea. The red heavy soils gives the extra punch in terms of strength and thickness of the brew. Today Ghatia is by far the most admired and sought after tea in the entire Dooars region. Ghatia also has the ability to produce flavoury orthodox during early March and CTC throughout the year.

Estate Address:

Ghatia T.E P.O. Nagrakata, DT. Jalpaiguri [...] This is an estate which is situated in the North Cachar of Assam, Chandighat has a predominance of Clonal tea and is known to produce bright coloury teas with adequate strength. This combination of brightness and colour with its superior strength makes Chandighat one of the most respected Tea Estate in Cachar. Chandighat has now become the household name and 50% of its produce is sold in the value added form of packets in areas in the vicinity of Cachar. Another 300,000 Kg are sold in value added form of packets in the highly quality conscious areas of Gujarat and Rajasthan. This shows its universal appeal and acceptability as a quality tea. Chandighat also produces quality green teas with high fibre content because it has a substantial quality of P-126 a quality orthodox done with a rich

## About Charaideo, Assam, India

Charaideo is a district in India, formerly part of Sivasagar district, separated out in 2015. Because the region is so new, it is not reflected on most maps and many tea gardens located in this new district are still listed in various sources as being located in Sivasagar.

title: Explore Historic Tea Gardens in Charaideo, Assam
content:
## History & Documentation Methodology

The establishment of tea gardens in Charaideo dates back to the mid-19th century, coinciding with British colonial expansion in India. The first tea saplings were introduced by Scottish planter Robert Bruce in 1823, leading to the commercial cultivation of Assam tea, which is now renowned globally for its robust flavor and distinctive aroma. The gardens were meticulously designed to optimize the microclimate for tea cultivation, utilizing the region's rich alluvial soil and favorable rainfall patterns. ## About Historic Tea Gardens

## Technical Overview

The Historic Tea Gardens of Charaideo, Assam, India, represent a confluence of agricultural innovation and colonial legacy, characterized by their unique landscape and biodiversity. These gardens not only serve as a crucial economic backbone for the region but also embody the intricate cultural narratives of Assam's tea heritage.

Assam CTC Tea is widely used not only in India but in the world. CTC tea is obtained by crush, tear and curl technique, hence CTC. In this technique, the black tea leaves undergo through a row of cylindrical rollers containing multiple sharp teeth used for crushing, tearing and curling leaves. The processed tea is then sent for packing or for loose selling in the market. Assam tea is famous for its fine variety because the tea is grown in favourable climatic conditions and ideal terrain. ### The Assam Difference: Why Geography Matters [...] ### Frequently Asked Questions

Do you deliver to Assam?▼

Yes, we deliver to Assam via our network of transport partners (Road/Rail). Delivery usually takes 3-5 days from Siliguri.

What is the minimum order quantity (MOQ)?▼

Our MOQ for wholesale orders is just 30 kg (one sack). You can mix different grades in your first order to test quality.

Can I get a GST bill?▼

Absolutely. We are a registered business and provide full GST billing for all interstate shipments to Assam.

title: Durrung Tea Estate
content:
+91 33 24757811
 admin@jalanindustries.com



## about us [...] Situated on the north bank of the mighty Brahmaputra, the Durrung tea plantation is one of the oldest tea estates in Assam. It was established in 1875 in the Darrang district of Assam. With a history of over 140 years, Durrung has always been known to produce premium quality black tea.  
  
The garden is located in the 2nd largest district of Assam - Sonitpur, with its headquarters in Tezpur. Tezpur is the IV Corp HQ of the Indian Army, has the Eastern Air Commander Base and is a utilitarian stopover for travellers to Arunachal Pradesh. The nearest station and airport to the garden is Rangapara railway station and Tezpur airport respectively.  
  
The Kaziranga and Orang wildlife sanctuaries, famous for the single-horned rhinos, are also in close vicinity of the garden. [...] ### the veg magic

title: [PDF] Addressing the Human Cost of Assam Tea
content: from THIRST, www.THIRST.international Estate tea sellers will go to centralized auctions and sell to brokers, who set prices based on the tea’s appearance, flavour, texture and fragrance. These brokers, in turn, sell to packers who sell the tea on to tea brands. Brands may also buy directly from estates or producer companies. These then find their way on to the supermarket shelf as part of well-known branded varieties or for ‘private label’ Assam tea – which is tea marketed under the name of the supermarket.37 13 Significant concentration remains in parts of the tea value chain. For example: • About 45% of Indian tea production is channelled through the auction system where only a small number of brokers – such as J. Thomas & Co Pvt Ltd (which handles 37% of tea auctioned in India, [...] & Co Pvt Ltd (which handles 37% of tea auctioned in India, Eastern Tea and Contemporary Brokers – are important players.38 • The packaging, branding and marketing stages of the tea value chain globally have also demonstrated high market concentration internationally. Just seven companies – Finlays, Ostfriesische Tee Gesellschaft, Tata Tetley, Twinings, Teekanne, Unilever and Van Rees – were estimated to account for 90% of tea traded into European and North American consumer markets in 2006.39 DO INDIAN, US AND EUROPEAN SUPERMARKETS SOURCE PRIVATE LABEL AND BRANDED TEA FROM ASSAM? The endpoint of the journey of Assam tea is our tea cups and mugs, both in India itself and around the world. Much of it – especially in Europe and the USA – is sold via supermarket shelves, either through [...] 20 Olympic-sized swimming pools.35 Of that, 15% is sourced from India. In Germany, another key market, some 18.7 billion cups of tea are consumed daily, largely of black tea varieties such as those from Assam.36 COMPLEX SUPPLY CHAINS AND HIGH LEVELS OF MARKET CONCENTRATION The way that tea is produced, traded, packaged and sold is complex – usually with multiple different companies involved before it reaches the consumer (see Figure 2). Production of tea in Assam continues to be dominated by large, private tea estates with in-house processing units. This is despite the upsurge in ‘small tea growers’ and independent BLFs as described in Section 1. 12 Figure 2: The Assam tea value chain Source: Created by Oxfam based on information from THIRST, www.THIRST.international Estate tea sellers

title: Global Tea Sourcing and Supply | Finlays
content:
FIND OUT MORE ABOUT OUR APPROACH TO SUSTAINABLE SUPPLY

Image 21

## Tea Solutions for a Range of Applications

Thanks to our global network of sourcing, blending and applications experts, we are able develop range of organic tea leaf solutions specific to your needs. Our tea is used in everything from loose tea, to the tea bag, to techniques such as Direct Leaf Extraction. We offer a vast range of packaging formats to meet your needs.

Image 23

Image 25

Image 27

## A LEADING PRODUCER AND SUPPLIER OF CERTIFIED LEAF TEA

Across different parts of our global business, we offer tea, coffee and botanical products and solutions with a range of certifications including Fairtrade, Rainforest Alliance, Organic, Kosher and Halal. Please get in touch to find out more.

## Get in Touch [...] FIND OUT MORE

## EXPERTS IN TEA SOURCING AND SUPPLY

With tea testing and tasting rooms across the world, our team of tea experts loves tasting, sourcing and supplying tea from around the world to meet your requirements. Whether it’s black and green teas from our farms in Kenya and Argentina, or Oolongs from China, Matcha from Japan or Darjeeling from India – we know tea and where to source the very best to capture unique regional flavours and profiles.

Our network of offices and gardens around the world, in major tea producing and consuming countries means we can service all of your tea requirements from bush to cup. This includes auction buying, bespoke blending, sourcing hard to find speciality teas from remote areas around the globe. [...] Our Businesses

content:
## Tea Garden (Jelam), Jonai, Dhemaji, Assam, India

Content updated on July 13, 2026.

#### Geographic Location of Tea Garden (Jelam)

The village Tea Garden (Jelam) is located in Jonai Circle of Dhemaji District in the State of Assam in India. It is governed by Kemi Jelem Gram Panchayat. It comes under Murkongselek Community Development Block. The nearest town is Pasighat (nt), which is about 31 kilometers away from Tea Garden (Jelam).

#### Travel and Communication for Tea Garden (Jelam)

The village is connected by public bus services. Private buses services are available for the village. There is a railway station 5-10 kms away from the village.

Postal Address:  
Tea Garden (Jelam),  
Dhemaji,  
Assam, India  
PIN - 787060 [...] Population density of Tea Garden (Jelam) is 1541.67 persons per square kilometer.

#### Land and Natural Resources in Tea Garden (Jelam)

Maps, reports and datasets of Tea Garden (Jelam) on topography, land use land cover(LULC), watershed / hydrological parameters etc. are available as a professional paid service. Please contact us for further details.

Total area of Tea Garden (Jelam) is 12 Hectares as per the data available for the year 2009.

About 2 ha is in non-agricultural use. About 3 ha is under miscellaneous tree crops.

About 3 ha is lying as current fallow area. About 3 ha is lying as fallow land other than current fallows. About 1 ha is covered by barren and un-cultivable land.

# Assam Tea (Wholesale)

brewing-information Description

Highly regarded by connoisseurs and casual tea drinkers alike, this tea is produced in the largest growing region in Northern India, Assam. This area of tea plantations has the perfect climate of rain and altitude that allow this iconic black tea to thrive each growing season, making it a popular choice for Assam tea bulk sourcing.

Its pungent liquor brews to a dark-reddish color, leaving a full-bodied cup with a malty taste that is truly unique. Assam takes well to milk and sugar; however, this robust style of tea tastes great brewed up and served as is. It is also a reliable option for retailers and cafés seeking consistentAssam tea wholesale supply.

Welcome to Zircon Tea Company, where every sip tells a story of exquisite craftsmanship and unparalleled flavor. Maps, reports and datasets of Chaibari Tea Estate on topography, land use land cover(LULC), watershed / hydrological parameters etc. are available as a professional paid service. Please contact us for further details.

Total area of Chaibari Tea Estate is 566.14 Hectares as per the data available for the year 2009.

Total sown/agricultural area is 501.8 ha. About 451.6 ha is un-irrigated area. About 50.2 ha is irrigated area. About 50.2 ha is irrigated by wells/tube wells.

About 8.7 ha is in non-agricultural use. About 45.46 ha is under miscellaneous tree crops.

About 1.37 ha is culturable waste land. About 2.05 ha is lying as fallow land other than current fallows. About 6.76 ha is covered by barren and un-cultivable land.

# Preetam Narayan Das - About Us

Preetam Narayan Das was established in the year 2021 in Dhubri, Assam, India. We are a supplier and trader of cooking coal and Indian cooking coal.

  

All our products are getting widely acclaimed among the large clientele for their exclusive designs, superior quality, and reliability. Apart from this, our ability to maintain timelines as well as quality in the assortment, providing cost-effective solutions and assurance to make timely shipment of the orders placed by customers have assisted us in positioning our name in the list of top-notch companies in the industry.

The products of our company are sourced from the best dealers. We ensure that these products are top-quality. We want our customers to get the best from us. All the products are packaged in quality packaging so that it reaches our customers safely and sound.

Why Us?

By providing a quality product and having experienced knowledge of the market, we have been able to attain a huge client base.

title: Damayanti Tea
content: Mr. Kishan Prithany, aged 44, is a dynamic entrepreneur whose vision, discipline, and diversified business acumen have shaped multiple successful ventures across Assam and beyond. A graduate in Commerce (B.Com) from Tinsukia College, Dibrugarh University, and an alumnus of Tinsukia English Academy, he brings both academic grounding and real-world insight into every enterprise he leads. Mr. Prithany formally entered the world of business in 2004, beginning his journey in the steel and cement sector. His strong leadership and ability to adapt to evolving market landscapes soon led him to expand into new industries. He entered the tea business in 2009, followed by the construction sector in 2015, and most recently, the hospitality industry in 2024, steadily building a reputation as a [...] Beyond his business accomplishments, Mr. Agarwala is a dedicated philanthropist with a strong sense of social responsibility. He has adopted four villages, actively working to uplift living conditions and support community development. His contribution to education is equally noteworthy — he has extended a valuable project grant to Dibrugarh University, benefiting the Departments of Sociology, Economics, and Statistics, and strengthening opportunities for academic research and student development. A leader with humility, purpose, and vision, Mr. Sunil Kumar Agarwala continues to inspire through his commitment to building meaningful enterprises and empowering communities. [...] Mr. Sunil Kumar Agarwala is far more than an entrepreneur — he embodies resilience, determination, and the remarkable journey of rising from modest beginnings to becoming a respected business leader. Educated in a vernacular-medium school in a small village in Assam, he has built his path through hard work, clarity of vision, and an unyielding commitment to progress. Over the years, Mr. Agarwala has successfully diversified his ventures across multiple sectors, including entertainment, malls, real estate, retail, and most significantly, tea. His leadership is marked by strategic thinking, bold expansion, and a deep understanding of emerging opportunities, which have collectively contributed to the sustained growth of his enterprises. Beyond his business accomplishments, Mr. Agarwala is a

## Orthodox Black Tea

‹ ›

### Description

Tea: Heritage Orthodox Black

Grower: Rajen Baruah of Heritage Tea

Origin: Dibrugarh, in Assam, India

Harvest Date: May 1, 2014

• • •

A classic Assam black tea, processed Orthodox style for full leaf and high-quality. Robust, assertive and strong, with malt, rose, and raisin flavors. The texture is full with a bright, astringent finish. [...] Currently, black tea from Assam is predominantly CTC (Crush, Tear, Curl), which is low-quality, fragmented tea. The region is dominated by large companies that make low-quality tea. Rajen Baruah had worked for these large tea companies, but came out of retirement to establish Heritage Tea. This Orthodox style of processing creates a more complex and deep flavor profile. He is now empowering Assam tea farmers to make specialty teas like these.

Heritage Tea grows with locally-sourced vermicompost, and is completely chemical-free.

• • •

Recommended Brewing Instructions: Use 1tsp (~3g) per 250ml cup.  
 Brew for 2 minutes.

- 195F for a less astringent, lighter profile with slightly sweeter notes.

- 205F for a fuller, sharper profile with deep raisin and malt flavors.

# Assam Tea Factory Capacity List

This document contains a list of 84 tea factories in Assam, India. It includes the factory name, type of tea produced (BLF = Black Leaf Fermented, EF = Orthodox Fermented), location, installed annual production capacity in kilograms, and any remarks. The factories are predominantly located in the districts of Jorhat, Cachar, Hailakandi, Karimganj, Dima Hasao, and Tinsukia and have a total estimated annual production capacity of over 800 million kilograms of tea.

## Popular Question and Answer

Can we come to visit your tea estate? [...] How can I arrange to come for a visit?

You can easily book your visit by contacting us through our website contact form, by email, or by giving us a call. For a smoother experience, please book at least one day in advance so we can prepare your tour and tasting.

Are there group tours available?

Yes, we offer guided tours for both individuals and groups. For groups of more than six people, please book ahead so we can arrange a private guide and ensure you have an unhurried experience.

What is the best time of year to visit?

The estate is beautiful year-round, but the tea-harvesting season (April to September) offers an especially vibrant atmosphere. During this period, you’ll see tea pluckers at work and the estate at its most active.

Can I buy tea directly while I am there? [...] Can I buy tea directly while I am there?

Definitely! You can purchase freshly packed Tsara Tea directly from our estate boutique. We offer our full range—from single-origin black teas to specialty blends—so you can take a piece of Ceylon home with you.

Is there tea tasting available?

Yes, tea tasting is one of the highlights of your visit. Our experts will guide you through the tasting process, explaining how aroma, color, and flavor vary across different types of Tsara Tea. It’s a sensory experience you won’t forget.

title: CTC Tea Full Form, History, Origins, Process, & Types Explained
content:
The history and origins of CTC tea are deeply intertwined with the rich tea culture of Assam. From its invention in the 1930s to its widespread popularity today, CTC tea has become a staple for tea lovers around the world. The innovation of the CTC process has made tea production more efficient and consistent, while the shift towards organic farming practices has added a new dimension to this beloved beverage.

Makaibari’s organic CTC tea embodies the best of both worlds – the robust, brisk flavour of traditional CTC tea and the purity and sustainability of organic farming. Whether you’re a long-time tea enthusiast or new to the world of tea, exploring the rich history and delightful flavours of CTC tea is a journey worth taking. [...] Assam tea, known for its malty and bold flavour, was particularly well-suited for the CTC process. The region's teas, including those produced at Makaibari, are renowned for their rich, full-bodied character. Today, Assam is the largest producer of CTC tea, with the majority of its tea processed using this method.

## The Process of Making CTC Tea

The CTC method is distinct from the traditional orthodox tea-making process. Here’s a step-by-step look at how CTC tea is produced:

1. Plucking: The process begins with the careful plucking of two leaves and a bud, the most tender part of the tea plant.

1. Withering: The leaves are spread out in withering troughs to reduce their moisture content by about 50%. This step is crucial for preparing the leaves for the CTC process. [...] By choosing Makaibari’s organic CTC tea, you are not only enjoying a delicious cup of tea but also supporting sustainable practices and the communities that produce this wonderful beverage. Brew a cup today and experience the rich legacy of CTC tea from the heart of Assam.

About Image

## About Us

Our journey began in 1977, when my father opened a small tea store in Golaghat, Assam. With a vision built on quality and trust, we started with loose tea. At just twelve, I joined him in promoting our tea to local towns.

In 1993, after completing my education, we introduced our own brand—Golden Eagle. As trained tea tasters, both my father and I aimed to perfect our blends based on customer preferences. Quality is our promise, not just a goal.

Today, our packaging operations are complemented by supplying premium loose tea on commission to renowned names in the industry: [...] Skip to content

## About Patco Plantations Private Limited

Patco Plantations Pvt. Ltd. - CIN U65992TN1955PTC002033 established in the year 1955, having a Registered Office at No.48, Vanguard House, 2nd Line Beach Road, Parrys Corner, Chennai – 600001. Our PAN is AAACP4367J and the GST number is 18AAACP4367J1Z5. We have two Tea Estates (Jamguri Tea Estate & Woka Tea Estate) in Golaghat, Assam. Both these Tea gardens have a combined Area of 1325 hectares (3275 Acres) with worker strength of 2500. title: About District | Hailakandi | Government Of Assam, India
content:
About District

Declared as the 24th district of assam in 1989 by a government of assam notification no. AAA.98/89/Pt/I Dt. 29/09/1989, Hailakandi is one of the aspirational districts in the barak valley region of Assam. Several myths are associated with the name ‘Hailakandi’.According to some historians the district got its name from the ‘Kuki’ word ‘halam’ means a small state and the word ‘Kundia’ meaning a plot of land for temporary ploughing. another myth associated with the name of the district is sali paddy is grown abundantly as a staple food crop and from ‘Sailkandi’ the district came to be known as Hailakandi.

# History [...] Perched in the heart of barak valley, Hailakandi was one of the oldest sub-divisions of assam constituted on 1st june, 1869. at the time of partition i.e. during 1946-47, Shri U.C. Roy, ACS was the sub-divisional officer of Hailakandi. prior to its emergence as a full-fledged district, Hailakandi was a civil sub-division under cachar district and when it became a district, Shri J.N. Sharma, IAS, DC Cachar was holding the charge of Hailakandi till 13th october 1989. Shri A. Mailk, ACS took over the charge of deputy commissioner of Hailakandi on october 14, 1989.Ddespite Hailakandi’s wearing a modern look, the district is rich in cultural heritage and tradition, it is in fact a melting pot of cultures and a converging ground of diverse caste, creed and religion. The district has glorious [...] the british raj in India, till the invasion of the British Administrators water way was the only means of connectivity in the district. During early british period changes in transport and communication system had taken place, the british rulers established many tea gardens and for transportation of raw and finished products, Hailakandi was linked with railway line. Gradually with the passage of time road communication developed in the district some scattered publications and public opinion highlighted the names of some managers of tea gardens viz. Mr. James Marshall Winchister, Mr. Mac Farlene Chalmers and M.R.Hoiates, the present chalmers higher secondary school in katlicherra was set up after the name of Mr. Mac Farlene Chalmers, the Graham M.V. school of Hailakandi was named after Mr.

###### Spice Lab / Warehouse [...] ##### History

Assam tea, like our Jorhat Assam, has a storied past, deeply rooted in the rich soil of India's Brahmaputra Valley. It was the British who commercialized tea cultivation in the region during the 1830s, using seeds smuggled from China. However, it was soon discovered that Assam had its indigenous tea variety, which was later classified as Camellia sinensis var. assamica. This variety is distinctively robust and malty, characteristics that define Jorhat Assam. Over the years, Assam tea has become a staple in many cultures, not just in India but around the world, adapting to various local tastes and preferences.

title: About tea industries | Government Of Assam, India
content: A tea garden was started by the Government in 1833 in erstwhile Lakhimpur district. With the arrival in London of the fine quality tea from this garden in 1938,the commercial circle of the city took a keen interest in tea plantations in Assam and a company known as the Assam Company was formed in 1839 to take over the experimental holdings of the East India Company's Administration over the tea gardens established in Assam till then. This was the first company in India to undertake the commercial production of tea and was, in fact, the direct successor of the East India Company. [...] A site was cleared from the jungle at Nazira which became and remained as the headquarters of this company until it was shifted to Calcutta in 1965. The official incorporation of this Company was effected in 1845. This company, however, did not attain much prosperity during the first ten years of its existence. By about 1852,under the management of George Williamson, one of the great pioneers in tea garden management, its condition began to improve and its success made the prospect of the industry so promising and attractive that speculators egarly rushed to it . [...] The tea industry in Assam is about 172 years old. It occupies an important place and plays a very useful part in the national economy. Robert Bruce in 1823 discovered tea plants growing wild in upper Brahmaputra Valley.

# Assam Orthodox Tea: Bold, Malty, and Exquisitely Crafted

#### About

Assam Orthodox Tea is a premium, hand-crafted tea made from the finest Assam tea leaves grown in the lush, fertile plains of India. This traditional orthodox tea is carefully processed using time-honored methods to preserve the full flavor and rich character of the leaves. With its bold, malty taste and deep amber color, it offers a robust and invigorating experience, making it a perfect choice for tea enthusiasts who enjoy a full-bodied cup.
